operator.note: Bridge from medicine's negative-results registry: institutionalize and reward the information in failures, which the binary accept/reject verifier currently incinerates.

question: On EinsteinArena, mandate that every verifier-rejected submission deposit a structured failure-region record (what was attempted, why it failed, what region of solution-space is now ruled out), and define a "discovery" as a rejected submission whose record measurably reduces later agents' wasted attempts in that region. Test whether a registry of certified dead-ends accelerates collective frontier progress more per-unit-compute than spending the same compute on additional frontier submissions.

floor.minimal_experiment: Run EinsteinArena (arXiv:2606.10402) on a fixed problem batch under three arms, each given an identical total compute budget C and identical agent population. ARM-A (control, status quo): all C spent on frontier submissions; rejections discard their reasoning, agents see only accept/reject. ARM-B (registry): on every rejection the agent emits a structured failure-region record (attempted approach + machine-checkable predicate describing the ruled-out region, e.g. a parameter box / proof-strategy class / solution-form constraint); later agents receive a retrieval of records relevant to their current target before submitting. Compute spent generating+indexing records is charged against C, so ARM-B makes strictly fewer frontier submissions. ARM-C (ablation / placebo-info): identical to B but the deposited record is a free-text rejection note with NO machine-checkable region predicate and NO targeted retrieval (records are dumped into a shared log agents may read but that is not indexed) — this isolates 'structured certified region-pruning' from 'agents just see more text'. MEASURED QUANTITIES: (1) per-unit-compute frontier progress = number of verifier-accepted novel solutions per unit C (primary); (2) wasted-attempt rate = fraction of submissions falling inside an already-deposited ruled-out region (the registry's mechanism-of-action probe); (3) time-to-first-solve per problem; (4) registry overhead = C fraction consumed by record generation/retrieval. Run >=20 seeds per arm; report effect sizes with CIs. FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION: ARM-B beats ARM-A on primary metric (1) by a margin exceeding the registry overhead (4), AND this gain is driven by a measurable drop in wasted-attempt rate (2) in B vs A, AND ARM-B beats ARM-C (i.e. the structured region predicate, not mere exposure to more text, carries the gain). The hypothesis is REFUTED if any of: B <= A on per-unit-compute progress; or B > A but B ~= C (the benefit is just 'more context', not certified pruning); or wasted-attempt rate does not fall in B (gain, if any, comes from somewhere other than the claimed mechanism). A secondary failure mode to log: the registry over-prunes — agents avoid a ruled-out region that actually contained the solution under a slightly different framing, raising time-to-first-solve on a held-out subset; if frequent, 'certified dead-end' is poisoning rather than accelerating.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Under equal compute, the registry arm (B) yields more verifier-accepted solutions per unit compute than the pure-frontier control (A) by a margin larger than the registry's own compute overhead, the gain is mediated by a measurable fall in the rate of submissions landing in already-ruled-out regions, and B outperforms the unstructured-note placebo (C). If B<=A, or B's advantage equals C's (mere extra context), or wasted-attempt rate does not drop, the claim is refuted.
floor.control: Two controls. Primary control ARM-A: identical compute budget and agent population spent entirely on frontier submissions with rejections discarded (status quo binary verifier) — isolates 'does logging failures beat just trying more'. Placebo control ARM-C: failures are logged as unstructured free-text into a non-indexed shared log with no machine-checkable region predicate and no targeted retrieval — isolates the load-bearing claim (structured certified region-pruning) from the confound (agents simply being fed more text).
floor.reject_reason:
[duplication] 这是 [1] (dof1) 的变体,攻击的是同一假设:平台的科学价值藏在"负空间"(被拒/被杀的尝试)里,而排行榜本体论抹掉了它;把失败显式建账并证明它比赢局更能驱动价值。机制对应关系:[1] 记录"被提议-却被拒"和"被退役为死路"的问题,造 anti-portfolio,测 KILL 决策质量是否预测平台价值;本提案记录"被验证器拒绝"的提交为结构化 failure-region 记录,造"认证死路登记册",测它是否每单位算力更快推进前沿。两者都是:为拒绝/死路建立显式台账 → 主张该台账是真正的价值/进度来源。差异仅是粒度(问题级 KILL 台账 → 提交级 dead-end 台账)和度量措辞(预测价值 → 减少后续浪费/每算力加速)的换皮——"把死路记下来引导后续 agent"正是 anti-portfolio 在操作上所做的事。没有可单独设计、能与 [1] 区分的独立机制:任何检验 [1] 的 KILL 台账价值的实验,本质上就在检验本提案的死路登记册价值。与 [3] (同为 dof2) 不同:[3] 作用于"被接受"的提交并认证泛化广度,对象不同;本提案作用于"被拒绝"的提交,对象与 [1] 重合。 ｜ [discriminating] REFUTED on three independent grounds; the design cannot discriminate the rival hypotheses as claimed.

(1) THE CONTROL IS A STRAWMAN — the discriminating contrast collapses. The proposal's ARM-A ("status quo: rejections discard reasoning, agents see only accept/reject") is NOT EinsteinArena's status quo. The paper (arXiv:2606.10402, Sec 2.6) describes a live discussion board whose explicit purpose is to "document failed approaches," "flag a numerical failure mode that others should avoid," and store "the path to the frontier" — i.e. a free-text dead-end registry already exists and is credited for the 593→604 kissing-number result. So the real platform is closer to ARM-C (unstructured notes) than to ARM-A. The experiment therefore does not test "registry vs. status quo"; it tests a fabricated baseline. The only genuinely informative contrast left is B vs C (structured predicate vs free text). But the falsification rule says the claim is refuted if B≈C — and that is the most likely outcome on these problems, so the design is biased toward a foregone refutation rather than a clean discrimination.

(2) THE EXPERIMENT IS NOT EXECUTABLE on this platform. The proposal demands an identical "total compute budget C," an "identical agent population," ≥20 randomized seeds per arm, and charging record-generation/retrieval "against C." The paper documents NONE of these primitives: agents register anonymously via proof-of-work with no human/operator disclosure, the platform is explicitly "in the wild," and there is no compute metering, no controlled population, no arm assignment, and no budget construct (grep for budget / per-unit / randomize / baseline / control arm = all absent). Worse, rejected submissions "are not stored in our database," so even the raw substrate the metrics need (rejection reasoning, wasted-attempt counts) does not exist and would have to be built for all arms. Running this requires the experimenter to OWN and METER the agents — which contradicts the platform's defining open/anonymous design. The data and compute control needed are not obtainable on the system being invoked.

(3) THE 'MACHINE-CHECKABLE RULED-OUT REGION' IS ILL-DEFINED for the actual problem class, making metric (2) and the B-vs-C contrast untestable as specified. Every active problem is continuous/combinatorial optimization (kissing number, circle/sphere packing, Thomson, autocorrelation integrals, Heilbronn). A verifier rejection means "this exact point scored below threshold δ," NOT "this neighborhood contains no solution." The paper's own kissing-number case study shows the valid 594-config was obtained by integer-snapping a near-valid configuration that, pre-snap, the verifier would reject — i.e. solutions sit arbitrarily close to rejected points in the same region. A sound, non-trivial region predicate (parameter box / solution-form class) that excludes failures but not nearby successes is essentially impossible to certify here: any region big enough to reduce later attempts will, with high probability, also contain a valid solution under a slightly different parameterization. The proposal files this ('certified dead-end poisoning') as a SECONDARY failure mode on a held-out subset, but for this problem class it is the GENERIC behavior, not an edge case. So 'wasted-attempt rate falls' (the claimed mechanism-of-action) and 'over-pruning raises time-to-first-solve' are confounded by construction: you cannot separately attribute a B-vs-A change to 'certified pruning helped' vs 'certified pruning poisoned and the residual is noise,' which is exactly the discrimination the experiment promises to deliver.

Net: the falsifiable prediction is not trivially-true, but the experiment that is supposed to test it is built on a non-existent control, requires compute/population controls and rejection-data the platform neither exposes nor permits, and rests on a region-predicate primitive that is unsound for the only problems available. It cannot cleanly distinguish 'structured region-pruning accelerates progress' from 'pruning poisons,' from 'mere extra context,' from 'no effect.' Refuted on testability/executability and on identifiability of the mechanism. ｜ [so_what] No named actor flips a concrete decision on this result. The supposed decider — the EinsteinArena operators choosing "build/mandate a dead-end registry vs. spend the compute on more frontier submissions" — gets no usable answer, for three reasons. (1) The experiment presupposes the only decision-relevant hard part: to "measure that a record reduces wasted attempts in a region" you must already have built the failure-region schema, an honest-deposit mechanism, and — critically — a verifier that can CERTIFY which region of solution-space is ruled out. That is strictly harder than certifying a single submission, and the source paper's central claim is that the scalar verifier is a liar (验证器≠真值). So the costly, build-or-not part is assumed, not tested; the A/B can't even run without first paying for it. (2) Both outcomes are non-actionable: if the registry wins, it merely re-confirms the millennia-old premise of literature reviews / negative-results journals (don't repeat known-failed attempts) — nobody updates; if it loses, it's confounded by this specific schema/incentive design, so no one concludes "dead-end registries don't work," only "this implementation didn't." (3) The frameshift project's own M3/v2 diagnosis already names the live命门 as the gate/verifier problem and lists "什么算发现" as an open self-referential question — this proposal restates that open question as a compute A/B without resolving the measurement primitive it depends on. Net: it is glue/restatement dressing "log negative results, they're useful" as an experiment, while quietly assuming an unbuilt region-certifying verifier. Deepens understanding / "worth studying" at best; no concrete invest, build, or believe decision gets reversed.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Operators/designers of open-ended multi-agent benchmark arenas (EinsteinArena and successors) and of LLM-agent scientific-discovery / automated-theorem-proving pipelines deciding how to spend a fixed compute budget; secondarily, RL-environment and self-play designers reasoning about reward for informative failure. NOT directly valuable to end users or to a near-term commercial buyer — the payoff is a design principle for research infrastructure, not a product.

prior_art.known_name: Nogood/conflict-driven clause learning (CDCL SAT solvers), tabu / no-good lists, and the pruning half of branch-and-bound — i.e. 'learning from certified failures to prune search space'. Also adjacent: experience replay of negative trajectories, and the real-world ClinicalTrials.gov / FDAAA-2007 negative-results-registry analogue the stimulus drew from.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known search-pruning mechanism (nogood/clause learning, branch-and-bound pruning) operationalized in a new setting: an open-ended multi-agent LLM discovery arena where the 'nogood' is a natural-language-checkable solution-region predicate shared across heterogeneous agents, and 'reduce wasted attempts per unit compute' is promoted to a first-class definition of discovery. The transfer is non-trivial because classical nogood learning assumes a formal, machine-decidable region predicate; whether fuzzy LLM-emitted failure-regions are crisp enough to prune without poisoning is the genuinely open question — but the core insight (failures carry prunable information worth rewarding) is decades old.

(1) THE CONTROL IS A STRAWMAN — the discriminating contrast collapses. Refuted on testability/executability and on identifiability of the mechanism.

No named actor flips a concrete decision on this result.
